# `conpty` sequences

<!-- Generated by `qdb generate reference` from `db/conpty.toml`. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Enable win32-input-mode (`conpty.win32_input.enable`)

Asks the terminal to report keyboard input as full Win32 key event records.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 9001 h`
- References: [Windows Terminal specs: #4999 — Improved keyboard handling in Conpty (win32-input-mode)](https://github.com/microsoft/terminal/blob/main/doc/specs/%234999%20-%20Improved%20keyboard%20handling%20in%20Conpty.md) (retrieved 2026-07-11)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/conpty/win32_input_enable.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/win32_input_enable.seq)

## Disable win32-input-mode (`conpty.win32_input.disable`)

Returns the terminal to sending ordinary key sequences instead of Win32 key records.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ? 9001 l`
- References: [Windows Terminal specs: #4999 — Improved keyboard handling in Conpty (win32-input-mode)](https://github.com/microsoft/terminal/blob/main/doc/specs/%234999%20-%20Improved%20keyboard%20handling%20in%20Conpty.md) (retrieved 2026-07-11)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/conpty/win32_input_disable.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/win32_input_disable.seq)

## win32-input-mode Key Event (`conpty.win32_input.key_event`)

Reports one Win32 keyboard event: virtual key, scan code, character, up/down, modifier state, and repeat count.

- Direction: terminal-to-host
- Syntax: `CSI Vk ; Sc ; Uc ; Kd ; Cs ; Rc _`
- Params: `Vk` (number), default 0, `Sc` (number), default 0, `Uc` (number), default 0, `Kd` (number), default 0, `Cs` (number), default 0, `Rc` (number), default 1
- References: [Windows Terminal specs: #4999 — Improved keyboard handling in Conpty (win32-input-mode)](https://github.com/microsoft/terminal/blob/main/doc/specs/%234999%20-%20Improved%20keyboard%20handling%20in%20Conpty.md) (retrieved 2026-07-11)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/conpty/key_a_down.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_a_down.seq), [`fixtures/conpty/key_a_up.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_a_up.seq), [`fixtures/conpty/key_ctrl_a_down.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_ctrl_a_down.seq), [`fixtures/conpty/key_left_arrow_down.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_left_arrow_down.seq), [`fixtures/conpty/key_f13_down.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_f13_down.seq), [`fixtures/conpty/key_emoji_surrogate_high.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_emoji_surrogate_high.seq), [`fixtures/conpty/key_emoji_surrogate_low.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_emoji_surrogate_low.seq), [`fixtures/conpty/key_repeat_a_down.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_repeat_a_down.seq), [`fixtures/conpty/key_degenerate.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_degenerate.seq), [`fixtures/conpty/key_all_empty_params.seq`](../../../fixtures/conpty/key_all_empty_params.seq)
- Notes: Terminal-to-host input, final byte `_`. All six fields are optional decimal parameters (omitted = default); `CSI _` is a legal degenerate event. Syntax is spec-normative from #4999, not a captured reply. A surrogate-pair code point spans two consecutive events (high then low half).
